note: Static, version-controlled reference data (snag S1: authored product data, NOT a frozen behavioral spec — editable; adding options/providers is pure data). sections -> fields (kind/severity/applies_when/custom) -> curated options (with requires/precludes gating). The five MVP field kinds only: text, single_select, multi_select, boolean, color. Severity is two-tier: recommended | optional (advisory — nothing is enforced; export is never blocked). Categorical option lists are authored alphabetically by label (None/Other sentinels last); inherently ordinal lists (billing_interval, asset_status) keep their logical order. Gating condition: { path, anyOf:[keys] } | { path, present:true }; an option carries `requires` (gated unless ALL hold) and/or `precludes` (gated if ANY holds); each rule carries its own `reason`.

sections.title: Identity & Scope
sections.blurb: What you're building, where it runs, who it's for, and how it makes money.

fields.note: The product's name — labels the manifest and the exported pack.
fields.label: Project name

fields.note: The kind of product you're building. Pick the closest match, or add your own via Other.
fields.label: Product type

fields.note: Where the app runs. Pick every target you ship to — it drives later platform decisions.
fields.label: Target platforms

fields.note: Who the product is for. Pick all that apply.
fields.label: Target users

fields.note: How the product makes money. A paid model unlocks the Payments section.
fields.label: Revenue model

fields.note: How often subscribers are billed. Only applies to recurring revenue models.
fields.label: Billing interval
Fulfilment
fields.note: What the customer receives. Pick all that apply, or add your own via Other.

sections.title: Frontend & Architecture
sections.blurb: The framework, rendering model, and visual styling the UI is built on.

fields.note: The framework the UI is built on, or add your own via Other. It gates compatible styling and component kits.
fields.label: Framework

fields.note: How the UI is styled. Options are filtered to what fits your framework.
fields.label: Styling approach

precludes.reason: Web CSS frameworks don't apply to a native mobile target — clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable web styling.
Emotion is a CSS-in-JS library for the web DOM and React Native — not available on a Flutter target.

requires.reason: NativeWind brings Tailwind to React Native — set a React Native or Expo framework.

precludes.reason: Panda CSS targets the web DOM ? clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable web styling.
Web CSS frameworks don't apply to a native mobile target — clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable web styling.
Web CSS frameworks don't apply to a native mobile target — clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable web styling.
styled-components targets the web DOM — not available on a Flutter target.

requires.reason: styled-jsx is used with React and Next.js ? set a compatible React framework.

precludes.reason: StyleX targets JavaScript UI stacks, not Flutter.
Web CSS frameworks don't apply to a native mobile target — clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable web styling.

requires.reason: Tamagui targets React and React Native — set a React or React Native framework.

precludes.reason: UnoCSS targets web build pipelines ? clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable it.
Web CSS frameworks don't apply to a native mobile target — clear the React Native / Flutter framework to enable web styling.

fields.note: A pre-built component kit, if any. Options are filtered to what fits your framework.
fields.label: Component kit

requires.reason: Angular Material requires Angular.
Ant Design requires a React framework.

precludes.reason: Ark UI targets web UI frameworks, not native mobile frameworks.
Bootstrap targets web UI, not native mobile frameworks.

requires.reason: Chakra UI requires a React framework.

precludes.reason: DaisyUI is a Tailwind CSS plugin for the web — not available on a native mobile target.
Flowbite is a Tailwind CSS component library for the web — not available on a native mobile target.

requires.reason: Fluent UI requires a React framework.
gluestack-ui targets React Native and Expo ? set a React Native or Expo framework.

precludes.reason: Ionic targets web/hybrid app stacks, not React Native or Flutter.

requires.reason: Mantine requires a React framework.
Material UI requires a React framework.
Quasar requires Vue.
Radix UI requires a React framework.
React Aria requires a React framework.
shadcn/ui requires a React framework (Next.js, React, React Router, or Astro).

precludes.reason: Shoelace / Web Awesome is a framework-agnostic web-component library (built on Lit) — it targets the web DOM, not a native framework.

requires.reason: Tamagui targets React and React Native — set a React or React Native framework.
Vuetify requires Vue.

fields.label: Native shell
fields.note: How a web frontend is wrapped for native distribution. Pick all that apply (e.g. Electron for desktop + Capacitor for mobile from one web codebase), or add your own via Other. Skip it if you're not shipping a native build.

requires.reason: Capacitor wraps a web app into a native mobile build — add an iOS or Android platform in Identity & Scope.

precludes.reason: Capacitor wraps a web frontend — it doesn't apply to an already-native framework (React Native, Expo, Flutter, Swift, Kotlin).

requires.reason: Cordova wraps a web app into a native mobile build — add an iOS or Android platform in Identity & Scope.

precludes.reason: Cordova wraps a web frontend — it doesn't apply to an already-native framework (React Native, Expo, Flutter, Swift, Kotlin).

requires.reason: Electron wraps a web app into a desktop build — add a macOS, Windows, or Linux platform in Identity & Scope.

precludes.reason: Electron wraps a web frontend — it doesn't apply to an already-native framework (React Native, Expo, Flutter, Swift, Kotlin).

requires.reason: Tauri wraps a web app into a desktop build — add a macOS, Windows, or Linux platform in Identity & Scope.

precludes.reason: Tauri wraps a web frontend — it doesn't apply to an already-native framework (React Native, Expo, Flutter, Swift, Kotlin).

sections.title: Data & Storage
sections.blurb: Where the app keeps its data, where files live, and the services that run background and realtime work. Picking a database, storage, jobs, or realtime provider derives its env vars into the exported .env.example.

fields.label: Database provider
fields.note: The platform or service that hosts your data — a DB host (Neon/PlanetScale/Turso), a bundled backend (Supabase/Firebase/Convex/Appwrite/PocketBase), or self-hosted. Selecting one derives its env vars into the exported .env.example.

precludes.reason: Precluded by Supabase as the backend. Clear Supabase to re-enable Firebase.

fields.label: File storage
fields.note: Where uploaded files and media are stored. Selecting a cloud provider derives its env vars into the exported .env.example.

requires.reason: Requires Appwrite as the backend.
Requires Convex as the backend.
Requires PocketBase as the backend.

fields.label: Background work
fields.note: The platform(s) you run background, scheduled, or async work on. Pick all that apply, or add your own via Other.

requires.reason: Appwrite Functions run on the Appwrite backend.
Convex Functions run on the Convex backend.

fields.label: Realtime sync
fields.note: The realtime / live-sync service (websockets, presence, collaboration). Many bundled backends include one; pick a dedicated service if your data layer doesn't, or add your own via Other.

requires.reason: Requires Appwrite as the backend.
Requires PocketBase as the backend.

fields.note: Your brand's primary accent colour — seeds the palette.

Primary font
fields.note: Heading / display typeface from Google Fonts, or add your own via Other. Picking one suggests a complementary secondary.

fields.label: Secondary font
fields.note: Body / supporting typeface. Auto-suggested from the primary; override any time, including via Other.
The icon set for interface and product icons. Add your own via Custom.
fields.label: Icons

sections.title: Surfaces

The legal and policy pages the product needs. Pick all that apply, or None.

sections.title: Assets
sections.blurb: Your brand-asset inventory and where the product sources stock media. The brand checklist is tracked only — it never enters staqpaq.yaml; the required-asset list is derived from product type and platforms, and ticking the checklist marks which of those files you already have.

fields.label: Brand checklist
fields.note: Which brand-asset files you already have. Tracked only — never written to staqpaq.yaml, but it marks each implied file in asset-checklist.md as have or still-needed. Leave one unchecked if you've yet to make it.

requires.reason: An app icon applies to installable targets (iOS, Android, a PWA, or a macOS/Windows/Linux desktop app) — add one in Identity & Scope.
A splash screen applies to mobile app targets — add iOS or Android in Identity & Scope.

fields.label: Stock assets
fields.note: Stock photo and video sources the product draws on. Selecting Other reveals an optional free-text field you don't have to fill in.
